title: taxus baccata subsp. minor
intro: Taxus baccata subsp. minor, also known as the Yew, is a small evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the Taxaceae family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Taxus baccata subsp. minor include its use in treating conditions such as arthritis, gout, and sciatica, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to note that the plant's seeds and leaves contain toxic compounds and should be handled with caution.
